extraction.one_line_summary: MADRAZO Ángel won after being dropped three times on final climb, LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel took red jersey finishing fourth after late attack.
tactical_patterns: MADRAZO repeatedly dropped then recovered on final climb before winning, revealing deceptive resilience over raw climbing power.
Burgos-BH placed two riders (MADRAZO, BOL) in break, allowing exhausted BOL to shelter final 10km for team 1-2.
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel attacked in final kilometer specifically to reclaim red jersey, not stage win, showing GC prioritization.
ROGLIČ Primož and VALVERDE Alejandro both conceded 7 seconds to LÓPEZ, indicating neither team willing to burn matches stage 4.
KUSS Sepp paced ROGLIC through finale, establishing Jumbo-Visma's stage race domestique pattern early in race.

rider_signals.MADRAZO Ángel: positive: Won despite appearing weakest climber in break, dropped three times but recovered, exceptional mental resilience under pressure.
rider_signals.BOL Jetse: positive: Sheltered final 10km after being in break all day, still finished second, excellent power management.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: Attacked final kilometer for red jersey, finished fourth, announced himself as GC favorite with strongest climb.
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: neutral: Lost red jersey but defended admirably, won respect with committed effort despite inevitable loss.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Conceded 7 seconds to LÓPEZ, appears conservative early race, not yet showing Grand Tour form.
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Paced ROGLIČ brilliantly through finale, establishing clear domestique hierarchy for Jumbo-Visma.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: Finished same time as ROGLIČ 7 seconds down, neither pushing hard suggesting wait-and-see GC tactics.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: Kept contention but didn't follow LÓPEZ attack, perhaps lacking explosive power of previous years.
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: neutral: Grimacing but hanging on with Colombian group, showing survivability not yet climbing dominance.
